identifierPolicies.label: Request version fingerprint
identifierPolicies.description: Task logical ids are derived from request lineage and the aggregate version hash.
identifierPolicies.versionRule: Logical id is stable per aggregate version; new version emits new versionId only.

identifierPolicies.label: Snapshot hash document key
identifierPolicies.description: DocumentReference identifiers are tied to the frozen evidence snapshot hash and artifact hash set.
identifierPolicies.versionRule: New snapshot hash emits a new DocumentReference version and supersedes prior set membership.

identifierPolicies.label: Transport correlation key
identifierPolicies.description: Communication identifiers bind dispatch or callback correlation tokens to the governing case and attempt.
identifierPolicies.versionRule: Duplicate transport receipts update the same logical id; semantic drift opens replay collision review.

identifierPolicies.label: External commitment case key
identifierPolicies.description: ServiceRequest identifiers bind one external commitment epoch to the governing booking or hub case.
identifierPolicies.versionRule: Authoritative commitment refresh increments versionId while preserving case lineage.

identifierPolicies.label: Pharmacy package fingerprint
identifierPolicies.description: Referral identifiers bind the frozen package hash, consent checkpoint, and selected provider tuple.
identifierPolicies.versionRule: Changing package hash, provider, or consent checkpoint forces new logical ids and plan supersession.

identifierPolicies.label: Consent scope fingerprint
identifierPolicies.description: FHIR Consent identifiers bind the chosen provider, scope hash, and consent checkpoint generation.
identifierPolicies.versionRule: Withdrawal or supersession issues a new version tied to the next checkpoint state.

identifierPolicies.label: Audit chain hash
identifierPolicies.description: Audit companion resources derive identifiers from the immutable audit chain hash and causal join token.
identifierPolicies.versionRule: Companion audit rows are append-only; they never mutate prior logical ids in place.

cardinalityPolicies.label: One task plus bounded document set
cardinalityPolicies.description: One Request aggregate version emits exactly one primary Task and zero or more supporting DocumentReference rows.

cardinalityPolicies.label: Snapshot document fan-out
cardinalityPolicies.description: One EvidenceSnapshot may emit many DocumentReference rows but all belong to one representation set hash.

cardinalityPolicies.label: Single communication per dispatch or callback epoch
cardinalityPolicies.description: Each dispatch or callback epoch emits at most one current Communication companion at a time.

cardinalityPolicies.label: Single commitment per case epoch
cardinalityPolicies.description: Each booking, hub, or pharmacy commitment epoch may emit one current ServiceRequest with companion resources.

cardinalityPolicies.label: Referral package set
cardinalityPolicies.description: Each pharmacy package hash emits one ServiceRequest plus bounded supporting Communication, Consent, DocumentReference, Provenance, and AuditEvent rows.

cardinalityPolicies.label: Single consent per checkpoint version
cardinalityPolicies.description: Each PharmacyConsentCheckpoint version emits one current Consent row with append-only supersession.

cardinalityPolicies.label: Single audit companion pair
cardinalityPolicies.description: Each immutable audit join emits one AuditEvent and one Provenance row only.

redactionPolicies.label: Reference-only artifacts
redactionPolicies.description: Payloads carry governed artifact refs, checksums, and masked descriptors only; no binary bodies or transcript text.

redactionPolicies.label: Minimal partner interchange
redactionPolicies.description: Only partner-required structured fields and declared profile elements may cross the boundary.

redactionPolicies.label: Callback correlation minimal disclosure
redactionPolicies.description: Inbound callback correlation exposes only partner correlation tokens, declared identifiers, and checkpoint posture.

redactionPolicies.label: Audit companion masked detail
redactionPolicies.description: FHIR AuditEvent and Provenance carry masked operator and actor descriptors while the immutable internal audit row stays authoritative.

companionArtifactPolicies.label: DocumentReference binary refs
companionArtifactPolicies.description: DocumentReference rows may reference Attachment object keys, checksums, and malware/quarantine posture but not inline payload bodies.

companionArtifactPolicies.label: Partner exchange manifest
companionArtifactPolicies.description: External bundles may carry an omission/redaction manifest that traces every excluded artifact or field to policy.

companionArtifactPolicies.label: Callback receipt refs
companionArtifactPolicies.description: Callback bundles must join to receipt checkpoints and replay-collision evidence instead of local receipt booleans.

companionArtifactPolicies.label: Dispatch manifest and omissions
companionArtifactPolicies.description: Pharmacy referral transport may include manifest evidence describing redactions or transport-specific omissions without rewriting the frozen canonical set.

companionArtifactPolicies.label: Audit hash join
companionArtifactPolicies.description: Audit companions must keep the immutable audit hash join and provenance linkage explicit.

replayPolicies.label: Stable set by aggregate version
replayPolicies.description: Replaying the same aggregate version rematerializes the same resource membership and identifier set.

replayPolicies.label: Stable docs by snapshot hash
replayPolicies.description: EvidenceSnapshot replay reproduces the same DocumentReference collection while snapshot hash inputs are unchanged.

replayPolicies.label: Stable callback set by transport correlation
replayPolicies.description: Duplicate or reordered callbacks target the same representation set until semantic drift forces collision review.

replayPolicies.label: Stable commitment by proof tuple
replayPolicies.description: Booking and hub commitment replay reproduces the same ServiceRequest membership until the authoritative commitment tuple changes.

replayPolicies.label: Stable referral by package hash
replayPolicies.description: The same pharmacy package hash, consent checkpoint, and provider tuple must rematerialize the same referral set.

replayPolicies.label: Append-only audit replay
replayPolicies.description: Audit companion replay may append new exports but may not silently fork prior audit companions for the same audit hash.

supersessionPolicies.label: Append-only by aggregate version
supersessionPolicies.description: New aggregate versions append new representation sets and explicitly supersede prior versions without mutating history.

supersessionPolicies.label: Snapshot replacement supersedes documents
supersessionPolicies.description: Superseding an EvidenceSnapshot invalidates the older DocumentReference membership while preserving immutable prior rows.

supersessionPolicies.label: Communication supersession on settlement revision
supersessionPolicies.description: Communication companions supersede only when the same dispatch or callback epoch receives a stronger settlement or contradiction.

supersessionPolicies.label: Commitment supersession on authoritative refresh
supersessionPolicies.description: Booking and hub ServiceRequest resources supersede only on authoritative refresh or cancellation evidence.

supersessionPolicies.label: Callback supersession on gate rotation
supersessionPolicies.description: Callback correlation representations supersede only when a new confirmation gate or correlation epoch replaces the old one.

supersessionPolicies.label: Consent invalidation on checkpoint drift
supersessionPolicies.description: Consent drift, withdrawal, or renewed checkpoint invalidates the prior current Consent row and requires a new representation set.

supersessionPolicies.label: Audit append-only companion
supersessionPolicies.description: Audit companions never rewrite the underlying audit truth; new exports append a new companion row with the same causal join.

callbackCorrelationPolicies.label: No callback correlation
callbackCorrelationPolicies.description: Clinical persistence-only contracts do not participate in partner callback correlation.

callbackCorrelationPolicies.label: Transport correlation keys
callbackCorrelationPolicies.description: Message or callback companions correlate via declared transport tokens and durable receipt checkpoints only.

callbackCorrelationPolicies.label: External confirmation gate correlation
callbackCorrelationPolicies.description: Callback representations bind to one current ExternalConfirmationGate and may not infer final success from transport acceptance alone.

callbackCorrelationPolicies.label: Dispatch attempt and outbound reference hash
callbackCorrelationPolicies.description: Pharmacy callback and proof correlation bind to the active dispatch attempt, dispatch plan hash, and outbound reference set hash.

prohibitedLifecycleOwners.blockedReason: Access grants are internal capability truth and may not surface as FHIR lifecycle owners.
source_refs: blueprint/phase-0-the-foundation-protocol.md#On the FHIR side, the mapping layer must now be explicit and replayable

prohibitedLifecycleOwners.blockedReason: CapabilityDecision is internal trust law and may not be flattened into FHIR status.
source_refs: blueprint/phase-0-the-foundation-protocol.md#On the FHIR side, the mapping layer must now be explicit and replayable

prohibitedLifecycleOwners.blockedReason: Session is local authority and never partner-visible FHIR lifecycle truth.
source_refs: blueprint/phase-0-the-foundation-protocol.md#On the FHIR side, the mapping layer must now be explicit and replayable

prohibitedLifecycleOwners.blockedReason: Lease ownership and takeover are control-plane facts, not FHIR statuses.
source_refs: blueprint/phase-0-the-foundation-protocol.md#On the FHIR side, the mapping layer must now be explicit and replayable

prohibitedLifecycleOwners.blockedReason: Reservation truth remains internal and may not be replaced by FHIR commitment state.
source_refs: blueprint/phase-0-the-foundation-protocol.md#On the FHIR side, the mapping layer must now be explicit and replayable

prohibitedLifecycleOwners.blockedReason: Browser/runtime parity tuples are release controls, not clinical resources.
Publication parity remains release control truth and may not leak into FHIR authority.
Trust and freeze posture are control-plane decisions, not FHIR-owned lifecycle state.

assumptions.statement: The seq_006 object catalog does not currently carry a first-class AuditRecord row, so seq_049 resolves AuditRecord authority from Phase 0 and the seq_048 audit event contracts until seq_053 publishes the authoritative audit ledger pack.

EvidenceSnapshot ownership is still marked `unknown` in the seq_006 catalog, so seq_049 anchors representation materialization to `intake_safety` because request snapshot freeze, attachment quarantine, and representation emission already converge there.
